originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 false)
     (= CONFIG_I2C true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 false)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Line 24 in ak4642_init(): `int codec_hw_write = i2c_master_send` — codec_hw_write is assigned but never read (dead store). The function ak4642_init() is compiled when SOC_AK4642=T, and i2c_master_send is declared when I2C=T; so the dead store occurs whenever SOC_AK4642=T AND I2C=T. The '// ERROR' at L24 marks the VBDB undeclared-identifier bug (I2C=F), but this DEAD_STORE alarm (I2C=T) is a distinct new bug not in VBDB. The PC adds FSI_AK4642=F, FSI_DA7210=F, SOC_DA7210=T — none of these affect ak4642_init(); the bug occurs for any value of those flags as long as SOC_AK4642=T AND I2C=T. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
